Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's motion for compromise of claim of Minor, Princess Fortenberry.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:56:09 a.m. and concluding at 10:32:01 a.m.

Plaintiff Vladimir Abramov appearing represented by Marvin Pederson, Esq., is placed under oath and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Regal Financial & Development Corporation not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
Supplemental Declaration re: the calculation of interest and request for attorney's fees filed Oct-17-2022.

Plaintiff requests interest from Nov-25-2022 to Oct-20-2022, at a rate of 9.75% and attorney's fees in the sum of $11,015.00.

VLADIMIR ABRAMOV is examined by the Court.
Pursuant to the default packet and statements submitted by Plaintiff, the Court finds sufficient evidence of prove breach of promissory note.

Court modifies dates of accrued prejudgment interest from Dec-23-2016 to Oct-20-2022 at a rate of 9.75 %. Plaintiff is entitled to prejudgment interest for a total of 2,127 days.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Vladimir Abramov, and against Defendant Regal Financial & Development Corporation, as follows:

1. Damages: $170,000.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $79,952.31
3. Attorney Fees: $7,806.72
4. Costs: $906.87

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $258,665.90

Proposed judgment signed and filed as modified.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2022 Continued To Sep-27-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jul-07-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MARVIN PEDERSON, Esq. (Tel: 415-785-8293) for plaintiff Vladimir Abramov;
Atty. JEFF WOO, Esq. of FTW Law Group (Tel: 213-335-3960) for defendants Regal Financial & Development Corporation and Hiram J. Woo.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS REGAL FINANCIAL & DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, AND HIRAM WOO's Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants REGAL FINANCIAL & DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION and HIRAM WOO's motion to vacate the default entered on December 2, 2020 is denied.

After the defaults were entered, the parties entered into a settlement agreement. (Abramov Decl., Ex. D.) Section 9 of the agreement provides:

9. MUTUAL RELEASE OF ALL CLAIMS. In consideration for their faithful performance of the terms of this Settlement Agreement, the parties, for themselves, their successors, assigns, and, do hereby relinquish, waive, release, acquit and forever discharge each other of and from any and all claims, disputes, actions, charges, contractual obligations, complaints, causes of action, rights, demands, debts, damages, or accountings of whatever nature, at law or in equity, known or unknown, asserted or not asserted, which they have now or may have in the future against one another, based on any actions or events which occurred prior to the date of this Settlement Agreement. (emphasis added.)

Based on the broadly phrased release, defendants waived any and all rights regarding service of the summons and complaint. The motion is therefore denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
